commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary: The PR adds internal-hook post actions, session-memory save blocking/content replacement, random-suffixed memory filenames, slug-generator hardening with disabled tools/random run IDs, and related docs/tests with a few incidental formatting-only test changes.

target.title: feat(hooks): postHookActions mechanism, blockSessionSave/sessionSaveContent, and slug-generation hardening
target.author: zeroaltitude

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: zeroaltitude
commands.command: — addressed the open maintainer-level questions you flagged on this pr. recent commits: **bd14845fa1 — public-surface docs, log clarity, privacy posture** 1. **public hook-runtime surface (`internalhookevent.posthookactions`).** previous jsdoc was a single line, inadequate for the api-compatibility / plugin-migration concern you raised. replaced with comprehensive docs covering: when to use the field, snapshot+clear drain semantics with explicit cwe-834 callout, per-action error isolation, optional/additive nature (existing handlers unchanged), and the explicit contract that actions pushed during drain are queued for the next cycle, not extended into the current one. plugin authors and maintainers now have the full contract in one place. 2. **premature save logging.** the pre-existing `memory file written successfully` / `session context saved to` log lines emitted before the post-hook drain decided whether to retract or replace the file — misleading audit trail. renamed to make staging vs final disposition explicit: `memory file written inline (pre-post-hook, may be retracted/replaced)` and `session context staged at <path> (final disposition decided by post-hook drain)`. operators grepping for save completion should filter on post-drain lines (`session save retracted` / `replaced`). 3. **late-blocksessionsave privacy semantics.** tightened the warning to match the loud-and-specific posture you asked for: - uses `warn` level with a `privacy:` prefix for grep-ability. - includes the transcript byte count so operators can size the egress. - explicitly states that retraction does not un-send the data from the slug-generation provider's logs / training pipeline. - re-emphasizes the mitigation (set `blocksessionsave` before the handler runs). **e812c0f923 — filename suffix entropy** (already pushed earlier today) 4. **4-hex character suffix entropy.** bumped from 4 hex chars (16 bits, ~50% birthday-paradox collision at 256 same-day same-slug saves) to 8 hex chars (32 bits, < 1 in 1e8 collision probability for realistic loads). test regex updated to match. **6f63e10f9d — cwe-834 self-append guard tests** (also already pushed) explicit regression tests for the snapshot+clear drain pattern, including a self-appending action test (with safety cap to prevent test runner hang) and a multi-handler stacking test. --- **on your split-the-pr suggestion** — i'd defer to maintainer preference. the three logical pieces (slug-generation hardening with `disabletools`, `posthookactions` mechanism, session-memory block/content controls with stronger filename uniqueness) are coherent enough to land together as one focused pr, and the test surface across them is interleaved (the post-hook callback's behavior depends on the inline write semantics, which depend on the slug-generation outcome). if you want them split, happy to do that — just say the word. **on testing readiness** — local `vitest run src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/ src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts` shows 70/70 passing. full suite at the relevant scopes is green. ready for fresh review.
commands.freeform_prompt: — addressed the open maintainer-level questions you flagged on this PR. Recent commits: **bd14845fa1 — public-surface docs, log clarity, privacy posture** 1. **Public hook-runtime surface (`InternalHookEvent.postHookActions`).** Previous JSDoc was a single line, inadequate for the API-compatibility / plugin-migration concern you raised. Replaced with comprehensive docs covering: when to use the field, snapshot+clear drain semantics with explicit CWE-834 callout, per-action error isolation, optional/additive nature (existing handlers unchanged), and the explicit contract that actions pushed during drain are queued for the next cycle, NOT extended into the current one. Plugin authors and maintainers now have the full contract in one place. 2. **Premature save logging.** The pre-existing `Memory file written successfully` / `Session context saved to` log lines emitted before the post-hook drain decided whether to retract or replace the file — misleading audit trail. Renamed to make staging vs final disposition explicit: `Memory file written inline (pre-post-hook, may be retracted/replaced)` and `Session context staged at <path> (final disposition decided by post-hook drain)`. Operators grepping for save completion should filter on post-drain lines (`Session save retracted` / `replaced`). 3. **Late-blockSessionSave privacy semantics.** Tightened the warning to match the loud-and-specific posture you asked for: - Uses `WARN` level with a `PRIVACY:` prefix for grep-ability. - Includes the transcript byte count so operators can size the egress. - Explicitly states that retraction does NOT un-send the data from the slug-generation provider's logs / training pipeline. - Re-emphasizes the mitigation (set `blockSessionSave` BEFORE the handler runs). **e812c0f923 — filename suffix entropy** (already pushed earlier today) 4. **4-hex character suffix entropy.** Bumped from 4 hex chars (16 bits, ~50% birthday-paradox collision at 256 same-day same-slug saves) to 8 hex chars (32 bits, < 1 in 1e8 collision probability for realistic loads). Test regex updated to match. **6f63e10f9d — CWE-834 self-append guard tests** (also already pushed) Explicit regression tests for the snapshot+clear drain pattern, including a self-appending action test (with safety cap to prevent test runner hang) and a multi-handler stacking test. --- **On your split-the-PR suggestion** — I'd defer to maintainer preference. The three logical pieces (slug-generation hardening with `disableTools`, `postHookActions` mechanism, session-memory block/content controls with stronger filename uniqueness) are coherent enough to land together as one focused PR, and the test surface across them is interleaved (the post-hook callback's behavior depends on the inline write semantics, which depend on the slug-generation outcome). If you want them split, happy to do that — just say the word. **On testing readiness** — local `vitest run src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/ src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts` shows 70/70 passing. Full suite at the relevant scopes is green. Ready for fresh review.
